Class Expense

java.lang.Object
Models.Expense
public class Expense extends Object
Represents a single expense entry recorded by the user.

Each expense belongs to a budget cycle and is classified under a category. It stores the amount, optional notes, a timestamp, and the PIN of the user who created it.

      private String userPin

  • Constructor Details

    • Expense

      public Expense(double amount, int categoryId, String notes)
      Constructs a new Expense with the given amount, category, and notes. The timestamp is automatically set to the current date and time.
      Parameters:
      amount - the expense amount (must be greater than 0)
      categoryId - the ID of the category this expense belongs to
      notes - optional notes describing the expense

  • Method Details

    • validateAmount

      public boolean validateAmount()
      Validates that the expense amount is greater than zero.
      Returns:
      true if the amount is valid; false otherwise

    • getExpenseId

      public int getExpenseId()
      Returns the unique identifier of this expense.
      Returns:
      the expense ID

    • setExpenseId

      public void setExpenseId(int expenseId)
      Sets the unique identifier of this expense.
      Parameters:
      expenseId - the expense ID to set

    • getCycleId

      public int getCycleId()
      Returns the ID of the budget cycle this expense belongs to.
      Returns:
      the cycle ID

    • setCycleId

      public void setCycleId(int cycleId)
      Sets the ID of the budget cycle this expense belongs to.
      Parameters:
      cycleId - the cycle ID to set

    • getAmount

      public double getAmount()
      Returns the monetary amount of this expense.
      Returns:
      the expense amount

    • setAmount

      public void setAmount(double amount)
      Sets the monetary amount of this expense.
      Parameters:
      amount - the amount to set

    • getCategoryId

      public int getCategoryId()
      Returns the category ID associated with this expense.
      Returns:
      the category ID

    • setCategoryId

      public void setCategoryId(int categoryId)
      Sets the category ID for this expense.
      Parameters:
      categoryId - the category ID to set

    • getNotes

      public String getNotes()
      Returns the optional notes attached to this expense.
      Returns:
      the notes string, or null if none

    • setNotes

      public void setNotes(String notes)
      Sets the optional notes for this expense.
      Parameters:
      notes - the notes to set

    • getTimestamp

      public Date getTimestamp()
      Returns the timestamp when this expense was created.
      Returns:
      the expense creation date and time

    • setTimestamp

      public void setTimestamp(Date timestamp)
      Sets the timestamp for this expense.
      Parameters:
      timestamp - the date and time to set

    • getUserPin

      public String getUserPin()
      Returns the PIN of the user who recorded this expense.
      Returns:
      the user's PIN string

    • setUserPin

      public void setUserPin(String userPin)
      Sets the PIN of the user who recorded this expense. Required by TransactionDAO.saveExpense() due to the NOT NULL constraint on the user_pin column in the expenses table.
      Parameters:
      userPin - the user PIN to associate with this expense

    • editExpense

      public boolean editExpense(double amount, int categoryId, String notes)
      Updates this expense with new values for amount, category, and notes.
      Parameters:
      amount - the new expense amount
      categoryId - the new category ID
      notes - the new notes
      Returns:
      true always, indicating the edit was applied

    • save

      public boolean save()
      Placeholder method for persisting the expense.
      Returns:
      true always
